mechanism_narrative: A flat or declining FEDFUNDS path lowers the discount rate on future earnings and reduces equity financing stress, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when rate cuts coincide with acute recession pricing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline after FEDFUNDS declines or if triggered periods produce negative returns in more than 55% of cases over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in FEDFUNDS above the neutral range tightens financial conditions, raises discount rates, and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when inflation is already elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS above 4.5% does not coincide with below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the joint FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL condition fails to outperform FEDFUNDS alone in out-of-sample ranking.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumption and revenue growth, which supports earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when sales gains are purely inflation-driven and not accompanied by real activity strength.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth does not predict higher 60-day SPX returns, or if the relationship weakens to near zero in periods where GDPC1 growth is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a short-run threshold signals deteriorating labor demand, weaker income growth, and higher recession probability, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is invalid when unemployment rises after a prior overheating episode but markets have already priced the slowdown.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE jumps above the trigger and SPX still delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of such instances, or if the signal disappears after controlling for PAYEMS trends.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating INDPRO captures stronger industrial output and better real-side momentum, which improves cyclical earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails when production rises only because inventories are being rebuilt after prior weakness.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising INDPRO do not show above-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative in more than one-third of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST signals housing demand and broader credit-sensitive spending, which leads equity returns over the next 60 trading days through higher construction activity and household wealth effects. The mechanism fails when housing starts rise while sales and labor data are weakening.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakouts do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint HOUST-RSAFS signal is not stronger than HOUST alone in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth turns negative while UNRATE is rising, labor-market deterioration signals a coming drop in household demand and earnings expectations, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days. The signal is strongest during confirmed employment downturns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls month-over-month and UNRATE rises
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE deterioration pattern fails to precede negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the signal is not statistically better than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A lower CPIAUCSL inflation trend combined with stable FEDFUNDS implies falling real policy rates and less inflation pressure on margins, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when inflation falls because demand is collapsing rather than normalizing.
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation episodes with stable FEDFUNDS do not lead to above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal underperforms a simple CPIAUCSL trend rule in walk-forward tests.
